title: Integration of Salmonella Testing Data into National Food Safety Dashboards
author: Sophian, Alfi
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Salmonella     remains     one     of     the     leading     causes     of     foodborne     illness     worldwide  ,   with     significant     public     health     and     economic     impacts  .   Integrating     Salmonella   testing data   into     national     food     safety     dashboards     offers     a     proactive     approach     to     outbreak     prevention     and     control  . By   consolidating     laboratory     results     from     farms  ,   processors  ,   and   retail   environments     into     a     unified   platform,   authorities     can     identify     contamination     trends  ,   respond     faster     to     emerging     risks  ,   and     allocate     resources     effectively  . Real-  time     visualization     of     test     results     supports   data-  driven     decision  -making   and     enables     targeted     interventions   in   high-risk     areas  .   This     integration     also     improves     transparency     for     stakeholders  ,   builds     consumer     trust  ,   and     supports     compliance     with     international     trade     standards  ,   ultimately     contributing     to     a     safer  ,   more     resilient     national     food     system  .
